review_title: High-Quality Albion® Magnesium — Great Absorption, Moderate for Sleep
review_text: I’d rate this a solid 5/5 for quality and formulation. This product uses Albion® TRAACS® magnesium lysinate glycinate chelate, which is known for its excellent bioavailability and stability. The chelation process binds magnesium to amino acids, helping it absorb well and reducing the likelihood of gastrointestinal issues, which makes it very reliable for daily magnesium intake. That said, while it’s a very good magnesium overall, it’s not the best option specifically for sleep. Because this is a lysinate–glycinate blend, the presence of lysine makes it slightly more neutral in effect compared to pure magnesium glycinate, which tends to have a more calming and sleep-promoting profile. In other words, it’s optimized more for absorption and tolerance rather than sedation or relaxation. Overall, this is a high-quality, well-absorbed magnesium that works well for general health, muscle function, and daily supplementation. If your main goal is improving sleep, a pure magnesium glycinate may be slightly better. But for quality, absorption, and consistency, this is still an excellent product and easy to recommend.

Magnesium bisglycinate is a highly absorbable form of magnesium in which the mineral is bound to the amino acid glycine. This chelated form is gentle on the stomach and less likely to cause digestive discomfort compared to other types of magnesium, making it a popular choice for daily supplementation. Magnesium is essential for hundreds of biochemical processes in the body, including muscle and nerve function, energy production, and the regulation of blood pressure. The bisglycinate form is often used to support relaxation, reduce muscle tension, improve sleep quality, and promote a calm nervous system. Because of its superior absorption, it is especially useful for people with digestive issues or those who struggle to maintain adequate magnesium levels through diet alone. It is generally considered safe, but taking very high doses may cause loose stools or interact with certain medications, so it’s best to use it under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

review_title: The only magnesium glycinate worth buying on here.
review_text: Guys, if you are taking magnesium for sleep and nervous system health you MUST check the back side!!! Most brands sell “magnesium bisglycinate” and when you look at the back you see “200mg elemental magnesium from magnesium busglycinate AND MAGNESIUM OXIDE” you do NOT know how much bisglycinate and how much oxide they put. And it will almost always be oxide because bisgycinate is a BIGGER MOLECULE, you cannot fit 200mg elemental magnesium from it into one pill. Oxide form doesn’t get absorbed at ALL, 4% bioavailability is useless, but its cheap and it is aimed at uninformed customers. This is a nuance most people don’t know, and I highly recommend this magnesium because I took 2 pills for the first time yesterday and I ACTUALLY wanted to sleep. This is the best form for raising your magnesium overall because this is the most bioavailable one, meaning less of it passes through to the intestines than what actually enters the bloodstream, PLUS there is the bonus of calming glycine which anyone benefits from. Malate is a good one but it’s stimulating, citrate is an okay one but still is mostly unabsorbed meaning it’s a better laxative + citrate source than a source of magnesium. Get this if you actually want systemic benefits, nervous system, muscles, joints, magnesium is needed everywhere and honestly 99% of people need this supplement because our foods don’t have the same mineral content anymore. Trust me you need this magnesium because it’s the best price:quality I have found on here. This is cheap for pure bisglycinate.

Here’s a balanced, detailed review of Doctor’s Best High Absorption Magnesium, based on user feedback, lab tests, and practical experience. If you want a short “pros & cons” list or summary later, I can pull that together too. What it is Doctor’s Best High Absorption Magnesium is a dietary supplement using a chelated form of magnesium (lysinate-glycinate) designed to improve absorption and reduce common side effects like digestive upset. It’s available in tablets or veggie capsules; different versions have different mg of elemental magnesium per dose.  It is marketed as non-GMO, gluten-free, soy-free, and vegan in many of its versions.  What people like about it From multiple reviews: Better sleep & relaxation Several users report improvements in sleep quality, fewer night awakenings, and better ability to relax. It seems particularly helpful when taken at night.  Muscle cramps / tension relief Many mention fewer cramps (legs, knees, etc.), less muscle tension or soreness after physical activity.  Gentle on the stomach Compared to magnesium forms like oxide (which often cause loose stools or GI discomfort), this one is frequently described as “gentle,” “no laxative effect,” or “easy to tolerate.”  Good value & quality assurance Users like that even though it’s a premium form (chelated), it performs consistently. There is also a Certificate of Analysis showing that heavy metals and microbiological parameters pass the safety thresholds.  What some don’t like, or trade-offs Large tablets in some versions A recurring complaint is that some of the tablet versions are large and difficult to swallow. For people who have trouble swallowing pills, that is a downside.  Dosage / cost efficiency Because of the formulation and “gentler” absorption, the elemental magnesium per tablet or capsule may be lower than some cheaper forms. Thus, to reach higher doses, you may need more pills — increasing cost.  Results can be gradual The effects (better sleep, reduced cramps) are often not dramatic immediately. Some users say they need consistent use over days to weeks to notice benefits.  Taste or pill texture issue Some reviews mention a slight taste or difficulty swallowing, especially with large tablets. For example, people switch to veggie capsules because the caps are easier to swallow.  Suitability & tips for using it Things that improve your chances of getting good results: Take with food, especially later in the day, if using for sleep/muscle relaxation. Be consistent; allow a week or more to see effects. Ensure adequate hydration. Magnesium works with other electrolytes and fluids. If other magnesium supplements gave you GI issues, this product might be a better option. My overall impression Doctor’s Best High Absorption Magnesium is a strong option for people looking for: A gentler magnesium supplement Better sleep, less muscle tension or cramps Good quality control and relatively low risk of side effects

Magnesium Review Magnesium is an essential mineral that plays a vital role in over 300 biochemical processes in the body. Here’s a detailed review of its benefits and characteristics: Key Benefits 1. Sleep Support: Magnesium is renowned for improving sleep quality by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system. It’s particularly beneficial for individuals with insomnia or restless nights. 2. Stress Reduction and Mood Stabilization: By regulating cortisol levels and supporting serotonin production, magnesium helps reduce stress and improve mood. It’s a natural aid for anxiety and emotional balance. 3. Craving Control: Magnesium can help curb cravings, especially for sugary foods, as it supports blood sugar regulation and promotes balanced energy levels. 4. Muscle Recovery and Energy Production: As a key player in energy metabolism and muscle contraction, magnesium aids in post-workout recovery and reduces muscle cramps or soreness. 5. Heart and Bone Health: Magnesium supports a healthy heart rhythm, aids in calcium absorption for bone health, and may reduce the risk of cardiovascular issues. 6. Versatile Forms: Magnesium is available in various forms (e.g., citrate, glycinate, oxide), each tailored for specific needs, such as digestion, relaxation, or general supplementation. Packaging, Size, and Taste Packaging: Magnesium supplements often come in capsules, powders, or liquid forms, with convenient and secure packaging. Look for reputable brands that ensure purity and quality. Size: Dosages vary by product, typically ranging from 100 to 400 mg per serving, depending on individual needs. Taste: Magnesium powders often have a mild, slightly metallic taste but are easily masked when mixed into water, juices, or smoothies. Capsules are tasteless and easy to consume. Final Verdict Magnesium is a must-have for anyone looking to support sleep, reduce stress, and enhance muscle recovery. Its versatility and broad range of health benefits make it an essential mineral for overall well-being. Whether you’re an athlete, someone with a hectic lifestyle, or just looking to improve your health, magnesium is a reliable and effective choice.

Magnesium is found throughout your body. Every cell in your body contains this mineral and needs it to function. About 60% of the magnesium in your body occurs in bone, while the rest is in muscles, soft tissues, and fluids, including blood . One of its main roles is to act as a cofactor — a helper molecule — in the biochemical reactions continuously performed by enzymes. It’s involved in more than 600 reactions in your body, including : Energy creation: converting food into energy Protein formation: creating new proteins from amino acids Gene maintenance: helping create and repair DNA and RNA Muscle movements: aiding in muscle contraction and relaxation Nervous system regulation: regulating neurotransmitters, which send messages throughout your brain and nervous system

review_title: Good value form of magnesium , especially if you suffer from anxiety or insomnia

review_title: Decent Magnesium Supplement but Didn’t Deliver Expected Benefits
review_text: I recently tried Doctor's Best High Absorption Magnesium (200 mg per serving) with the hope of improving deep sleep and alleviating foot cramps for myself and restless leg for my partner. While the product itself seems well-formulated and provides half of the daily recommended magnesium intake, we didn’t experience the benefits we were looking for. After consistent use, there was no noticeable improvement in our sleep quality or in my foot cramps. It's possible that the dosage wasn’t sufficient for our specific needs, but based on our experience, this supplement didn’t meet our expectations. If you're looking for a magnesium supplement to support sleep or muscle health, you might want to try other options on Iherb. I wouldn’t purchase Doctor's Best Magnesium again or recommend it for these specific purposes.

The magnesium glycinate was effective, but I was hoping for more relaxation from it. Surprisingly, after three months of taking it, I noticed it actually increased my anxiety. However, once I stopped taking it, the anxiety ceased as well. While it didn’t meet my expectations in terms of relaxation, it’s worth noting its impact on anxiety levels for others considering this supplement.

Key Health Benefits of Magnesium: Muscle and Nerve Function: Aids in muscle contraction and relaxation, reducing cramps and twitching. Bone Health: Supports bone density, as a significant amount of the body's magnesium is stored in bones. Mental Health and Sleep: Regulates neurotransmitters to calm the nervous system, which can help with anxiety, depression, and insomnia. Energy and Metabolic Health: Converts food into energy, enhances insulin sensitivity, and helps manage type 2 diabetes risk. Headache Relief: May reduce the frequency of migraine headaches.  Common Forms and Sources: Food Sources: Leafy greens (spinach), nuts (almonds), seeds, legumes, and whole grains. review_text: This magnesium supplement features Albion® TRAACS® chelated lysinate glycinate, delivering 100 mg per tablet with 240 tablets per bottle – a generous 2-4 month supply depending on your dose. The tablets are small and easy to swallow, with no bitter aftertaste common in other magnesium products.  Quality & Ingredients: The chelated form ensures exceptional absorption and bioavailability, meaning more magnesium is utilized by the body rather than passing through the digestive system. Unlike magnesium oxide or citrate, this formula is gentle on the stomach and rarely causes digestive discomfort. It’s free of gluten, dairy, soy, and artificial additives, with Albion’s patented technology guaranteeing purity and potency.  Performance & Benefits: Users report improvements in sleep quality, muscle relaxation, stress management, and reducing leg cramps. It also supports heart health and bone density, making it a well-rounded choice for overall wellness. The consistent dosing makes it easy to adjust intake based on individual needs.  Value & Formulation: While slightly more expensive than non-chelated magnesium, the high absorption rate means you can take a lower dose to achieve the same benefits, offering good long-term value. The tablet form is more convenient than powders or liquids for daily use.  A minor consideration: as with any magnesium supplement, it’s best to take it with food and avoid pairing it with calcium-rich meals to maximize absorption. Those with kidney issues should consult a healthcare provider before starting.  Overall, this is a premium magnesium supplement that stands out for its superior absorption and gentle formulation.

Magnesium is the brother of calcium, which should be twice as much in the body as magnesium. There is a misconception that we suffer from calcium deficiency and as a result, for example, our bones break. In reality, bones break because they do not have enough magnesium, which ensures bone flexibility. Calcium accumulates mainly in our body, because it is mainly found in drinking water. This calcium is inorganic and interferes with the functioning of the body. Magnesium ensures the dissolution of inorganic calcium and its distribution to the bones together with vitamin D3 + K2 or its elimination from the body. Inorganic calcium causes, for example, hemorrhoids, varicose veins and calcification of the heart valves. Its excess on the one hand and deficiency on the other will cause the body to clog up like a kettle. High-quality magnesium can solve this problem. Doctor's Best High Absorption Magnesium, 100 mg, 120 Tablets, is a dietary supplement known for its high bioavailability and effectiveness in supporting overall health. It utilizes magnesium chelated with amino acids (glycinate and lysinate) for optimal absorption and minimal gastrointestinal discomfort. This formulation is particularly beneficial for individuals who may have difficulty absorbing magnesium from standard sources. Magnesium is essential for numerous biochemical processes in the body, including muscle and nerve function, energy production, and maintaining cardiovascular health. Each tablet provides 100 mg of elemental magnesium, allowing for easy adjustment of dosage based on individual needs or healthcare provider recommendations. The suggested dosage is 2 tablets twice daily, totaling 400 mg daily, which can be adjusted according to personal health requirements. Doctor's Best is committed to quality and safety, ensuring their supplements are tested for purity and potency. The product is free from common allergens like gluten and GMOs, catering to diverse dietary preferences. User feedback often highlights benefits such as improved muscle relaxation, enhanced sleep quality, and overall well-being with consistent use. Some users may find it more comfortable to take the supplement with food to avoid mild gastrointestinal effects that can occur when taken on an empty stomach. In summary, Doctor's Best High Absorption Magnesium offers a well-tolerated and highly absorbable form of magnesium that supports various aspects of health. It is suitable for individuals seeking to supplement their diet with magnesium to promote daily wellness and maintain optimal bodily functions. As with any new supplement regimen, consulting with a healthcare professional is recommended, especially for those with existing health conditions or taking medications.
